turns.content: I am BEGGING the city of Chicago to give me a break with this heat oh my god
Ha, is the Chicago summer hitting hard right now? What's the temperature situation looking like?

it's been like 97 degrees for FOUR DAYS STRAIGHT with that horrible humidity that makes it feel like 110 and I literally cannot function. like the air is soup. you walk outside and immediately your clothes are wet
Ugh, that Midwest humidity is something else. It turns the whole city into a steam room. Are you able to stay in AC most of the time, or are you having to be outside a lot?

my apartment AC is this tiny window unit from like 2003 that sounds like a jet engine and barely cools one room so I've basically been living in my bedroom with the door shut like a prisoner. and the CTA?? taking the L in this heat should be classified as a human rights violation the platforms have zero shade and you're just standing there marinating

The L platforms in summer are truly awful — all that concrete and metal just radiating heat back at you. Have you thought about getting a second window unit, or is that not an option with your apartment setup? Even a good portable fan can help circulate the air from the one AC you have into other rooms.

my landlord is so useless about this stuff. I asked about central air when I moved in and he literally laughed at me. LAUGHED. sir this building was built in 1920 I understand but also people live here in 2025 and we deserve to not melt. I might get a second window unit but they're all sold out everywhere because obviously everyone in Chicago waited until the heat wave to buy one

Classic Chicago move — everyone panic-buying ACs during the first heat wave. Check online retailers that can ship to you in a day or two, or even Facebook Marketplace sometimes has people selling barely-used units. In the meantime, the wet towel on the back of the neck trick actually works surprisingly well, and if you put a bowl of ice in front of your current fan it creates a poor man's AC for the other rooms.

the ice bowl thing is so ghetto but honestly I might try it at this point lol. and the worst part is everyone from like California or Texas is always like "oh you don't know REAL heat" like ok but we also had a polar vortex six months ago where it was NEGATIVE THIRTY so we get the extremes in BOTH directions excuse me we suffer year round

That's actually such a good point — Chicago gets hit from both ends. You go from frostbite warnings in January to heat stroke warnings in July. At least in places with consistent heat, people have infrastructure for it. Chicago buildings are built to trap heat for winter, which makes summer in those older apartments even worse.

EXACTLY thank you!! the buildings trap heat because they were designed for winter so in summer my apartment is basically an oven. like the radiator isn't even on but the walls are warm to the touch. I love this city but the weather is genuinely unhinged. we get maybe 3 weeks of nice weather in may and then it's either freezing or boiling there is no in between

The three weeks of perfect Chicago weather in spring are what keep everyone going through the other 49 weeks of suffering, honestly. For your apartment though — if the walls are radiating heat, blackout curtains on the sun-facing windows can make a huge difference. They block the solar heat gain, which is probably half the problem.

ok I actually do have blackout curtains already for sleeping during the day and yeah they help a little bit but it still feels like I'm living inside a slow cooker. whatever I'm gonna go fill my bathtub with cold water and sit in it like a sad soup. thank you for letting me rant I just needed to yell into the void about this weather lol

A cold bath honestly sounds like the best plan right now! Hang in there — heat waves break eventually, and you'll get some relief soon. And definitely check online for that second window unit before the next wave hits. Stay cool out there!

honestly the worst part is I know in like 5 months I'm going to be complaining about the cold again and wishing for summer. the cycle never ends with this city lol we are always suffering and yet I would never leave

That's the Chicago paradox right there — you complain about it 365 days a year but would fight anyone who says a bad word about the city. It's a toxic relationship with the weather, honestly. But hey, at least you've got good food, great neighborhoods, and the lake. Enjoy your cold bath, and maybe set a reminder to order that window unit before the next heat wave. Future you will thank present you.
